sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to cast, to throw, Exo 15:1, 21 Exo 15:21. (2) to shoot (with a bow), Jer 4:29 Psa 78:9. (Arab. رمى Eth. ረመየ፡ Syr. and Ch. ܪܡܳܐ id. To this answers Gr. ῥίπτω.)

Piel רִמָּה to beguile, to deceive (prop. to throw down, to trip up, like the Gr. σφάλλω; whence Lat. fallo), Pro 26:19 Gen 29:25. Pregn, 1Ch 12:17, לְרַמֹּותַנִי לְצָרַי “to deceive me (and betray) to my enemies.”

Derivatives, רְמִיָּה, מִרְמָה, הָּרְמָה, תַּרְמִית, and pr.n. יִרְמְיָה.

רמה

f. (with Kametz impure, from the root רוּם to be lofty)-

(1) a lofty place, 1Sa 22:6 especially one consecrated to the worship of idols, Eze 16:24, 25 Eze 16:25, 39 Eze 16:39. Compare בַּמַה.

(2) [Ramah], pr.n.-(a) of a town in the tribe of Benjamin, Jud. 19:13 with the art. Isa 10:29 to the north of Jerusalem, Jos 18:25 Jud. 4:5 Jer 31:15 Hos 5:8 1Ki 15:17.-(b) of a town in Mount Ephraim, the birth-place and abode of Samuel, 1Sa 1:19 2:11 7:17 15:34 16:13 fully רָמָתַיִם צֹופִים 1Sa 1:1, Gr. Ῥαμαθέμ, 1Ma_11:34 .-(c) of a town of Naphtali, Jos 19:36.-(d) רָמַת הַמִּצְפֶּה (“the high place of the watchtower”), Jos 13:26, a town in Gilead, otherwise called רָאמֹות, רָמֹות, verse Jos 13:9.-(e) רָמַת לְחִי, see לְחִי No. 3 . Gentile noun רָמָתִי 1Ch 27:27.
